statement.markdown: Given a 2D grid of size n x m where each cell is either 0 (obstacle) or 1 (free), find the number of paths from the top-left cell to the bottom-right cell. The player can only move right or down at any time.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be provided through the stdin. The first line will contain two space-separated integers, n and m. The next n lines will contain m space-separated integers representing the grid.
statement.outputFormat: Output the number of paths from the top-left cell to the bottom-right cell.
statement.notes: The player can only move right or down at any time. If the top-left cell is an obstacle, the program should return 0.

constraints.description: Size of grid, number of rows
Size of grid, number of columns

examples.explanation: There are two paths from the top-left cell to the bottom-right cell with the given grid.

hints.text: First hint text, remember to check for the base case
